title: Congress accuses Rajnath Singh of misleading Parliament on Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Centre’s disclosure exposes a contradiction between the Defence Minister’s statement in Parliament and the official record, the Opposition party says, calling for a full disclosure of facts in the monsoon session

title: BJP rule in Bengal will be no different from rest of country: Venugopal
content: They do not believe in the Constitution. They want to destroy the livelihood of the common people, says Congress leader K.C. Venugopal

title: K.C. Venugopal moves privilege notice against Rajnath for ‘misleading’ Parliament about Operation Sindoor casualties
content: The Congress leader said that during the discussion held in July 2025 regarding the Pahalgam terror attack and subsequent Operation Sindoor, Rajnath Singh had told the House that no harm had come to Indian soldiers

title: Decks cleared for implementation of ₹12,642-crore VB-G RAM G in Tamil Nadu on July 1
content: As part of the funding arrangement, which is 60:40 for the Union and State governments, the former’s contribution will be ₹ 7,585 crore and the latter’s ₹ 5,057 crore, said an official in the State government.

title: Over 20 lakh devotees attend Rottela Panduga in Nellore
content: On the occasion, State Endowments Minister Anam Ramanarayana Reddy offered special prayers at the dargah

title: Three killed as two lorries collide, catch fire in Andhra Pradesh’s Guntur
content: One of the lorries was transporting stones, while the other was carrying a paper consignment, says Guntur District Superintendent of Police Vakul Jindal.

title: Sahitya Akademi Award winner Puviyarasu dead
content: DMK president M.K. Stalin and CPI(M) State secretary P. Shanmugam condoled his death, recalling his immense contribution to Tamil literature and modern poetry

title: Ankita Bhandari case: Uttarakhand HC refuses interim relief to convicts
content: Seeking suspension of sentence and bail, the convicts, Pulkit Arya and Saurabh Bhaskar, argued that Ankita had died by suicide and that there were no direct eyewitnesses to the incident

title: 4-year-old child who fell into borewell in Haryana’s Ambala dies
content: The Deputy Commissioner had said on Tuesday (June 30) that the police had been directed to initiate action as per rules against those responsible for leaving the borewell open

title: Petrol, diesel margins back above pre-conflict levels: Report
content: The start of the West Asia conflict triggered a surge in global oil prices but retail pump rates in India remained steady for large parts and rising only by a fraction

title: Need for inclusive, integrated climate action
content: Climate and heat considerations should be integrated into occupational health policies for municipal and contracted sanitation workers

title: White Paper puts Kerala’s liabilities at ₹5.07-lakh crore, CM says fiscal structure under ‘serious’ strain
content: Salaries, pensions, and interest payments consumed 77.6% of the revenue receipts in 2025-26; a major revamp of KIIFB recommended; urgent reforms sought in KSEB, KSRTC, and KWA; State’s capital expenditure at 1.3% of the GSDP is “one of the lowest” among Indian States

title: The fiscal tightrope for State governments
content: Most State government expenditure is on social sectors, such as health and education, and economic sectors, such as agriculture and irrigation. In Kerala, such spending has driven social progress since the 1960s. The gap between expenditure and receipts is usually financed through market borrowings.

title: Vellayil to get a facelift with two major projects
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan announced the allocation of funds in his reply to the 2026–27 Budget presentation recently

title: West Bengal govt. to introduce Uniform Civil Code Bill soon
content: Committee led by retired judge of Supreme Court Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai to study State’s family laws and submit report in four weeks; tribal communities to be excluded from the law

title: ₹523-crore project cleared to tackle Kochi’s water shortage
content: The Minister said steps had been taken to address the damage to the tank and make temporary arrangements to ensure water supply.

title: Tamil Nadu’s elusive Bengal foxes and their shrinking space
content: Bengal foxes are known for their monogamous behaviour. Forest Department officials said the species are now known to occur only in pockets in Dharmapuri, Tiruvannamalai, Salem, Madurai and Villupuram.

title: Higher conviction in cases after full implementation of new criminal laws: Home Minister Shah
content: The three new justice-based criminal laws are one of the milestones achieved by the country in the last 11 years, he said

title: Ram Temple donation theft tests VHP’s demand to free temples from state control
content: Describing government control of temples as a ‘threat to Hindu society’, the VHP had launched a nationwide campaign in 2021 to free Hindu temples and religious institutions from government control

title: All eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case sent to judicial custody
content: Despite rumours that Champat Rai has resigned as general secretary of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, no official confirmation yet; arrested accused include close aides of Mr. Champat Rai

title: Champat Rai: The RSS pracharak at the centre of the Ram temple donation row
content: After decades as a low-profile organiser working behind the scenes, Mr. Rai has been thrust into an unwelcome spotlight amid corruption charges and rumours of his resignation from the Ram temple trust

title: Ayodhya Temple donation theft will have long-term consequences for the Sangh Parivar
content: The sordidness of the embezzlement issue has led to questions about the integrity of those who spearheaded the movement to build the Ram Temple in place of the Babri Masjid, with the loss of a moral high ground for the BJP and the Sangh Parivar

title: Left parties to skip Vijay’s invitation for meeting of post poll allies
content: The leaders of the two Left parties, however, would meet the Chief Minister at Secretariat and bring to his attention many pressing issues of the workers and other sections of the society.

title: Why are States opposing VB-G RAM G | Explained
content: While the Bill increases the number of guaranteed workdays from 100 to 125, it also significantly raises the financial burden on States from the current 10% share to 40% of total expenditure

title: U.P. Congress delegation allowed to pray at Ram Temple after face-off with Ayodhya administration
content: “An impartial investigation is not happening,” State chief Ajay Rai said on the temple donations embezzlement case

title: INDIA bloc writes to CJI on ‘biased conduct’ of EC; DMK, AAP sign letter
content: The letter has been signed by 23 parties, including the AAP and DMK, which did not participate in the June 8 INDIA bloc meeting in Delhi; Jairam Ramesh says “Opposition parties are firmly anchored”

title: Ayodhya Bar boycott: Supreme Court has said even the ‘wicked’ have the right to a lawyer
content: A 2010 judgment of the Supreme Court, refers to Article 22(1) of the Constitution, which mandates that an arrested person should not be ‘denied the right to consult, and to be defended by, a legal practitioner of his choice’

title: U.P. Congress chief, MPs stopped from visiting Ram Temple in Ayodhya, put under house arrest
content: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai says he was arrested from his hotel and is being detained in a State-run university guest house; Amethi MP asks why the right to darshan is being denied on political grounds

title: CBI arrests IAS official in Haryana govt. funds misappropriation case
content: CBI has so far filed charge-sheets against 17 accused in the Haryana case, including six bank officials of IDFC First Bank and AU Small Finance Bank, three public servants, two companies and six private individuals

title: Deep trust deficit stalling Siang mega dam in Arunachal Pradesh
content: Despite several potential benefits, local communities are opposing the subcontinent’s largest planned dam on the Siang river. Why is there resistance, and what does the government need to do build trust?

title: State orders twin probes into Davangere hospital oxygen cylinder blast
content: The State Health Department has ordered two separate inquiries into the oxygen cylinder explosion inside the operation theatre of the Chigateri District Hospital in Davangere

title: Jaishankar speaks with Ireland Foreign Minister, discusses ways to deepen ties
content: Ireland will take on one of the EU's most influential roles when it assumes the Presidency of the Council of the European Union from July 1 to December 31, 2026

title: Court convicts EMPEE’s Shaji Purushothaman in 2013 fatal drunk driving case
content: A 13-year-old boy was killed and five persons were injured in the accident involving the car Shaji drove. The prosecution alleged that none of the accused had attempted to aid the injured

title: India and Japan to renew focus on Indo-Pacific for the first visit by Prime Minister Takaichi
content: Ms. Takaichi’s visit, that marks the 20th annual summit since India and Japan begun to meet regularly at the highest levels and is expected to set the course for ties over the next decade

title: Criminal justice system’s digital push aims for a full roll-out by next year
content: Rollout of ICJS, which will integrate police, courts, prisons, forensics, and prosecution on one platform, will enable end-to-end digital workflow; data will be stored in government-owned cloud platform MeghRaj

title: Road restoration works planned across four GCC zones
content: Restoration works in areas such as Sholinganallur and Madipakkam, where multiple utility projects are under way, are expected to be completed by September, an official added.

title: Godrej Properties acquires 47-acre land parcel in South Chennai, eyes ₹500 crore revenue
content: Godrej Properties already has four developments in Tamil Nadu, excluding the project it has currently signed

title: Commercial LPG cylinder prices slashed by Rs 183.50 from July 1: Check city-wise rates
content: Commercial LPG prices have seen a significant drop of Rs 183.50 effective July 1, bringing relief to businesses like hotels and vendors. A 19 kg cylinder now costs Rs 2930 in Delhi.  Meanwhile, prices for household cylinders remain unchanged. This follows a price hike in June attributed to supply issues.

title: 'The numbers don't add up': Karnataka minister grills officials over stray dogs, missing trees; watch
content: Bengaluru's development minister, Krishna Byre Gowda, expressed dismay over the ineffectiveness of the Animal Birth Control program, questioning how Rs 100 crore spent has yielded only marginal reduction in stray dogs. He also demanded answers regarding the disappearance of over 11 lakh saplings planted in two decades. Officials struggled to provide satisfactory explanations, prompting the minister to call for scaled-up sterilization and transparent tracking of tree plantation survival rates.

title: France script unique FIFA World Cup feat, become first team to ...
content: France has etched their name in FIFA World Cup history, becoming the first team to score three or more goals in five consecutive matches after a dominant 3-0 victory over Sweden. Kylian Mbappe was once again the star, netting a brace and climbing the all-time scoring charts. Coach Didier Deschamps expressed pride in his team's consistent performance as they advance to face Paraguay in the Round of 16.

title: Nayara Energy cuts petrol by Rs 5 per litre, diesel by Rs 3
content: Nayara Energy, India's largest private fuel retailer, has slashed petrol prices by Rs 5 and diesel by Rs 3 per litre nationwide. This marks the first price reduction by any fuel retailer in over two years, driven by easing Middle East tensions and falling international crude oil prices. While Nayara passed on the benefits, state-run companies kept rates unchanged.

title: USCIS clarifies foreign national registration requirement; Who it applies to
content: USCIS has finalized a new online registration and biometrics rule for certain foreign nationals in the US. This policy, effective since April 2025, targets those lacking standard immigration records. Individuals entering without inspection, certain visa-exempt Canadians, and 14-year-olds in the US must register. The agency assures no new groups are affected, emphasizing procedural updates and traveler-friendly changes to an existing system.

title: NIA arrests ex-TMC MP Aparupa Poddar’s husband in 2023 Ram Navami violence
content: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) arrested Shakir Ali, a Trinamool councillor and husband of former MP Aparupa Poddar, in connection with the 2023 Ram Navami violence in Rishra. Ali was detained after investigators found inconsistencies in his statements regarding clashes during a BJP MLA's rally. His arrest has sparked protests, with his wife also facing charges for allegedly obstructing police duties. The NIA is investigating the conspiracy angle.

title: Scorpio seized over 154 liquor bottles; HC quashes confiscation over lack of proof
content: Chhattisgarh High Court has ruled that vehicles cannot be confiscated under the Excise Act solely on the assumption of exceeding legal liquor limits. Chief Justice Ramesh Sinha dismissed the state's plea, upholding the release of a Scorpio. The court found no proof that seized liquor bottles surpassed the legal threshold, emphasizing the need for concrete evidence over mere assumptions for confiscation.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: US is already deploying weapons in western Pacific to defend Taiwan from China
content: The US is bolstering its defenses in the Indo-Pacific, deploying advanced weapon systems like NMESIS and MADIS in Okinawa and the Philippines. These mobile missile systems, positioned strategically near Taiwan, aim to deter Chinese aggression by challenging maritime traffic and aerial threats. This forward deployment is a key component of the US strategy to defend Taiwan and maintain regional stability.

title: If doctors wrote prescriptions for themselves, here's what would be on them
content: On India's National Doctors' Day, four physicians reveal their personal health strategies, emphasizing small, consistent habits over perfection.  From mindful eating and daily movement to prioritizing sleep and social connections, these healers demonstrate that sustainable well-being is achievable. Their insights underscore the importance of self-care for those who dedicate their lives to others, offering a realistic blueprint for a healthier life.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has exited New York City real estate at a steep loss, selling his stake in struggling landlord A&E Real Estate for just six cents on the dollar. The Bloomberg-reported sale lands as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze on one million rent-stabilized apartments squeezes landlords already battered by 2019 rent laws, soaring costs and mounting unpaid rent.

title: FIFA WC: Mexico end 40-year knockout curse, beat Ecuador 2-0 to reach last 16
content: Mexico have finally broken their four-decade World Cup knockout jinx, defeating Ecuador 2-0 to advance to the Round of 16. Goals from Julián Quiñones and Raúl Jiménez sealed the historic victory, ending an eight-game streak of eliminations. The win, despite a weather delay, marks Mexico's first knockout success since 1986, with young Gilberto Mora and veteran Jiménez setting personal milestones.

title: 4-year-old trapped 220-ft inside borewell in Ambala dies after 19-hour rescue operation
content: A tragic 19-hour rescue operation in Haryana's Dhanaura village concluded with the recovery of a four-year-old child from an open borewell, but the toddler was declared brought dead at the hospital.  Despite extensive efforts by multiple agencies, including the Army and NDRF, challenges like rising water levels and unstable soil hampered the rescue. The open borewell has since been sealed.

title: 'We've got our tactics': Harry Brook on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi challenge
content: All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as India faces England in the first T20I.  The young batting sensation, who had a record-breaking IPL season, could become India's youngest international debutant, surpassing Sachin Tendulkar's record. England captain Harry Brook acknowledged the team has prepared extensively for the possibility of facing the talented left-hander, with specific tactics devised to counter him.

title: Can landlords charge tenants for repainting walls? What Delhi High Court said
content: Tenants aren't always responsible for repainting or minor repairs upon moving out. A Delhi High Court ruling clarified that landlords must distinguish between normal wear and tear, which they cover, and actual damage caused by tenants. While routine painting is the landlord's duty, significant damage like removed fittings can be charged to the tenant, provided the landlord proves it and apportions costs fairly.

title: CBSE Class 10 second board exam results soon: What is the best marks rule students must know?
content: CBSE's Better Marks Rule, introduced under the new two-board examination system for Class 10 from the 2026 academic session, allows students to retain the higher score from two board exam attempts. Applied separately to each subject, the rule offers a chance to improve marks or clear failed papers while reducing exam pressure and eliminating the risk of losing existing scores.

title: Are Samay Raina and Medha Shankr dating? Viral video sparks fresh speculation
content: Comedian Samay Raina and actress Medha Shankr from '12th Fail' are fueling new dating rumors after being spotted together in a video that has gone viral. Observers noticed their attempts to dodge paparazzi while enjoying each other's company. This isn't their first brush with romance speculation, as previous public appearances have also led fans to wonder about their potential relationship, which remains unconfirmed by either party.

title: Throwing eggs not our culture, it will tarnish party’s image across India: BJP West Bengal chief
content: BJP state president Samik Bhattacharya has strongly condemned the act of throwing eggs, stating it's not the party's culture and urging members to refrain from such actions that could damage the party's national image. He also cautioned against arrogance following recent electoral success, emphasizing that the public rejected the TMC's hubris. Bhattacharya further called for an investigation into government spending on lawyers.

title: At least 5 killed, 8 injured as bus falls off Agra-Lucknow Expressway after collision with car
content: Tragedy struck Unnao as a speeding sleeper bus veered off the Agra-Lucknow Expressway, claiming five lives, including two children. The bus collided with a car before plunging onto the road below. Preliminary reports suggest the bus driver may have fallen asleep at the wheel. Eight others were injured and are receiving medical attention. Authorities are investigating the exact cause of the devastating accident.

title: China keeps up pressure on Taiwan; 13 PLA sorties of PLA aircraft, 3 ships detected near territory
content: Taiwan reported a significant Chinese military presence with 13 aircraft sorties and 10 naval vessels operating near its waters, with nine aircraft crossing the median line. This comes after Taiwan's combat readiness drills, designed to counter potential surprise attacks. Beijing criticized the exercises, while Taiwan continues to bolster its defenses, including its first domestically built submarine undergoing sea trials. The situation highlights ongoing tensions over Taiwan's self-governing status.

title: 40°C and counting: Europe was built to beat the cold. Now heatwave is exposing its biggest weakness
content: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. Buildings that sheltered against frosty temperatures have turned into sweltering traps, revealing a serious flaw in adapting to intense summer heat. With infrastructure buckling under record temperatures and a surge in heat-related fatalities, a fundamental reevaluation of urban planning is desperately needed to face an increasingly hotter reality.

title: Tensions flare at Manipur border as security forces stop Kuki march over Naga economic blockade
content: Tension flared on the Imphal West-Kangpokpi border as Kukis attempted to march towards Namdilong, protesting a prolonged economic blockade by Naga groups. Security forces intervened, preventing the march. The blockade, enforced by Naga organizations seeking justice for six slain civilians, has severely impacted essential supplies to Kangpokpi. Both communities remain on edge amidst escalating demands for action and justice.

title: CBSE Class 12 supplementary exam 2026 announced; Schools asked to submit LOC by July 8
content: CBSE has begun the registration process for the Class 12 Supplementary Examination 2026, directing affiliated schools to submit the List of Candidates (LOC) through the Pariksha Sangam portal between June 30 and July 8. Eligible compartment and improvement candidates can apply, while students omitted from the LOC will not be permitted to appear for the examination.

title: Days after Pakistan's airstrikes, Afghanistan launches strikes on ISIS targets in Balochistan
content: Afghanistan's Taliban forces conducted strikes in Pakistan's Balochistan province, targeting alleged Islamic State bases. This follows Pakistan's recent airstrikes in Afghanistan, which killed civilians and were condemned by India as a "blatant act of aggression." Tensions are escalating after a period of relative calm, with intermittent border clashes and air strikes claiming dozens of lives recently.

title: Warriors make contract move for 7-foot-2 star amid LeBron James rumors
content: The Golden State Warriors re-signed center Kristaps Porzingis to a two-year, $40 million contract structured as an extension, a detail that makes him immediately trade-eligible even though the team is expected to keep him. The move comes as Golden State pursues free agent LeBron James, who confirmed this week he's leaving the Los Angeles Lakers after eight seasons. James is the betting favorite to sign with the Warriors, where he'd join Stephen Curry and Jimmy Butler on a roster featuring three of the NBA's highest all-time earners.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chargers, Panthers linked to $90m Commanders star
content: Daron Payne's future with the Commanders hangs in the balance as he enters the final year of his substantial contract. With a new coaching staff and defensive scheme, his 2026 performance will be crucial. Several teams, including the Chargers, Jaguars, Panthers, and Lions, are reportedly monitoring his situation and could pursue him if he hits free agency next spring, seeking to bolster their defensive lines.

title: 'Coaches & players were informed': FIFA breaks silence on VAR ruling after Germany's exit
content: FIFA has defended a controversial VAR decision that disallowed Germany's extra-time goal against Paraguay in their World Cup exit. Referees chief Pierluigi Collina stated the call aligned with new guidelines punishing attackers obstructing opponents without playing the ball.  Germany legend Thomas Muller vehemently criticised the decision, calling it "daylight robbery" and expressing frustration with technology's impact on the game.

title: 'No one is more disappointed than I am': Koeman resigns after Netherlands' historic WC exit
content: Ronald Koeman has resigned as Netherlands head coach after their surprising FIFA World Cup 2026 exit to Morocco. This marks the end of his second tenure, with the Dutch suffering their earliest-ever World Cup elimination. Koeman cited the disappointment and a desire to prioritise family and health as reasons for his departure, expressing gratitude for his time with the squad.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for July 1, 2026 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: NACL Industries, Info Edge (Naukri), and Minda Corporation - these are the top stocks to buy recommended by Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares for July 1, 2026:

title: As VB - G RAM G comes into force today govt notifies enhanced wage rates
content: A new rural employment framework, Viksit Bharat- Guarantee for Rozgar & Ajeevika Mission (Gramin), has launched with a significant wage hike. Daily wages now stand at a minimum of Rs 300, with the national average rising to Rs 327.4. This revision, exceeding 10% nationally, prioritizes states with historically lower wages, aiming to boost rural incomes and foster inclusive development.

title: Mbappé shatters World Cup history, breaks 20-year record and now chases Messi
content: Kylian Mbappé scored twice as France beat Sweden 3-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32, breaking multiple records in the process. He became the all-time leader in World Cup knockout goals and overtook Miroslav Klose to move second on the overall scoring list. France now advance to face Paraguay, while Mbappé closes in on Lionel Messi’s record.

title: Doctor who runs for tiny hearts: How Dr Satwik turned fitness into mission to save newborn lives
content: Dr. Vikas Satwik, a Bengaluru-based neonatologist, finds running essential for his demanding profession. Inspired by charity marathons in the UK, he now runs weekly to maintain physical and mental readiness for unpredictable emergencies. His participation in a 10K run supports the Neonatal Foundation of India, an organization he credits with significantly improving newborn care outcomes and lives. Dr.

title: 7 dead, 15 injured as bus catches fire after collision on Delhi-Mumbai Expressway
content: Tragedy struck the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in Dausa early Wednesday as a private bus veered off the road into a gorge after colliding with a trailer, claiming at least seven lives and injuring over 15. The accident, occurring between 2-3 am, saw the bus catch fire, intensifying the panic. Rescue efforts were swift, with the injured receiving immediate medical attention. An investigation into the cause is underway.

title: 'Ready for war': Iran draws red line for US, says no final talks until MoU terms are met
content: Iran's Parliament Speaker has issued a stark warning of war if the US fails to adhere to a memorandum of understanding, emphasizing that negotiations will halt until Washington fulfills its commitments.  Tehran views recent Persian Gulf incidents as violations, vowing a strong reaction. Discussions are currently focused on implementing the MoU, including the lifting of a naval blockade and the release of frozen assets, with technical talks planned in Doha.

title: Petrol, Diesel Get Cheaper At Nayara Pumps As Global Oil Prices Ease
content: Public sector oil marketing companies such as Indian Oil, Bharat Petroleum and Hindustan Petroleum haven't announced any reduction in retail fuel prices.

title: Why US Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Ruling Is A Relief For Indians
content: Hundreds of thousands of Indian professionals work and study in the country on H-1B visas, L-1 intra-company transfer visas, and F-1 student visas, while more than a million Indians remain in the employment-based green card backlog

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Trade Flat Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent oil crude rose 0.5% to $73.32 while US WTI rose 0.59% to $69.91 per barrel.

title: Video: Month Before Murder, Ketan Agarwal Seen Dancing With Fiancee Siya Goyal's Parents
content: Ketan Agarwal is seen happily dancing with his and his fiancee Siya Goyal's parents at a family function.

title: Six-Figure Salary, Empty Wallet? Why India's High Earners Feel Trapped
content: Wealth creation is driven more by consistency than by income levels alone. Investing regularly often matters more than waiting for the perfect salary.

title: Commercial LPG Gets Cheaper, No Relief For Households Yet
content: Commercial LPG Price Cut: The price of the standard 14.2-kg domestic LPG cylinder has been left unchanged across the country.

title: Jharkhand Man Stabbed For Protesting Harassment, Dies. Massive Protests Follow
content: A man, who was critically injured in a stabbing for protesting the alleged harassment at a bar in Jharkhand's Jamshedpur, died during treatment, triggering massive protests.

title: 7 Killed, 22 Injured As Bus Crashes, Catches Fire On Delhi-Mumbai Expressway
content: The incident took place around 2:30 am last night when the bus was travelling from Rishikesh to Madhya Pradesh's Indore at high speed.

title: 25-Year-Old Hindu Priest 'Kidnapped', 'Tortured' For Ransom In Bangladesh
content: Deuri -- a first-year student at Central Law College in Old Dhaka, who also occasionally serves as an assistant priest at the central temple of Jagannath University -- was targeted by unidentified persons late on Monday.

title: Airlines Baggage Mishandling Down By 23%, Still Costs $6.3 Billion: Report
content: With net profit averaging just $8 per passenger, one mishandled bag wipes out the profit from more than 30 seats sold, the report said.

title: Vijay's Alliance Outreach Sparks Buzz Over New Secular Front In Tamil Nadu
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay to host allies for a tea meeting to discuss a broader secular alliance.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Monsoon Likely To Reach Delhi Around July 3-4
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) said that the monthly average rainfall over the country as a whole during July 2026 is most likely to be below normal.

title: US Lifts Restrictions On Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5 AI Models
content: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday.

title: NASA Rolls Out More Moon Base Plans
content: NASA on Tuesday announced new uncrewed missions to aid in the future creation of a lunar surface base, a project beginning to take shape despite recent setbacks.

title: Delhi's EV Policy 2.0: What It Means For Legacy 2-Wheelers And Repair Shops
content: Delhi EV Policy 2.0: Major two-wheeler makers will have to compete with EV-focused companies that have been building electric since inception.

title: Microsoft To Cut Under 2.5% Of Workforce In Latest Layoffs: Report
content: Microsoft is planning to cut under 2.5% of its workforce in the latest round of layoffs that could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.

content: DMK and AAP have signed the letter to the CJI despite not being part of the INDIA bloc . the two parties did not attend the meeting of the opposition alliance on June 8 . a joint letter signed by 23 political parties plus one independent has been sent to the cJI today .

title: Which drug works best for you? One blood test can tell
content: folic acid is usually prescribed to someone with high levels of homocysteine . but when a standard course barely lowered the numbers in a patient, genetic analysis revealed he carried a gene variant that affected his ability to convert it into its active form . around 30% of people carry this genetic variation, something that doesn’t show up in regular blood tests .
